title: On A Very Old Glass At Market-Hill
content:
Frail glass! thou mortal art as well as I;
        Though none can tell which of us first shall die.


    ANSWERED EXTEMPORE BY DR. SWIFT

    We both are mortal; but thou, frailer creature,
        May'st die, like me, by chance, but not by nature.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Jonathan Swift delivers a powerful performance in "On A Very Old Glass At Market-Hill"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
